preview.summary: A potentially decisive Group G finale pits Mohamed Salah's Egypt against an organized, experienced Iran. With knockout spots on the line, expect a tense, tactical contest between two of the World Cup's most seasoned non-European sides.
preview.homeForm: Egypt topped their African qualifying group comfortably, scoring 19 goals in nine matches with Salah netting nine.
preview.awayForm: Iran qualified for a fourth straight World Cup, winning seven of ten games to top their Asian group and arrive ranked 21st.
preview.homeKey: Mohamed Salah, closing in on Egypt's all-time scoring record.
preview.awayKey: Mehdi Taremi, Olympiacos striker with 10 qualifying goals.
storylines: Salah and Taremi headline a clash of attacking stars
Iran arrive without omitted striker Sardar Azmoun
Both nations chasing a rare World Cup knockout berth
preview.h2h: Egypt and Iran have met just twice, most recently a 3-0 Iran win in 2003, and have never met at a World Cup.
